apa_citation: Campion, M. A., Fink, A. A., Ruggeberg, B. J., Carr, L., Phillips, G. M., & Odman, R. B. (2011). Doing competencies well: Best practices in competency modeling. Personnel Psychology, 64(1), 225-262. https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1744-6570.2010.01207.x
notes: Best-practice HR competency modeling source; supports observable behaviors and KSAO-based modeling.

apa_citation: Campion, M. C., Schepker, D. J., Campion, M. A., & Sanchez, J. I. (2020). Competency modeling: A theoretical and empirical examination of the strategy dissemination process. Human Resource Management, 59(3), 291-306. https://doi.org/10.1002/hrm.21994
notes: Directly links competency models with strategy dissemination and employee behavior.

apa_citation: Bos-Nehles, A., Townsend, K., Cafferkey, K., & Trullen, J. (2023). Examining the Ability, Motivation and Opportunity (AMO) framework in HRM research: Conceptualization, measurement and interactions. International Journal of Management Reviews. https://doi.org/10.1111/ijmr.12332
notes: Recent HRM review of AMO conceptualization, measurement, and interactions.

apa_citation: Jiang, K., Lepak, D. P., Hu, J., & Baer, J. C. (2012). How does human resource management influence organizational outcomes? A meta-analytic investigation of mediating mechanisms. Academy of Management Journal, 55(6), 1264-1294. https://doi.org/10.5465/amj.2011.0088
notes: Top-journal meta-analysis linking HRM practices to ability, motivation, opportunity, and organizational outcomes.

apa_citation: Ployhart, R. E., & Moliterno, T. P. (2011). Emergence of the human capital resource: A multilevel model. Academy of Management Review, 36(1), 127-150. https://doi.org/10.5465/amr.2009.0318
notes: Foundational multilevel model of individual human capital emerging into unit-level human capital resources.

apa_citation: Ployhart, R. E., Nyberg, A. J., Reilly, G., & Maltarich, M. A. (2014). Human capital is dead; long live human capital resources! Journal of Management, 40(2), 371-398. https://doi.org/10.1177/0149206313512152
notes: Clarifies human capital resources as collective, strategically valuable resources.

apa_citation: Gerhart, B., & Feng, J. (2021). The resource-based view of the firm, human resources, and human capital: Progress and prospects. Journal of Management, 47(7), 1796-1819. https://doi.org/10.1177/0149206320978799
notes: Connects RBV, HR, and human capital; useful for strategic value of talent capabilities.

apa_citation: Ray, C., Essman, S. M., Nyberg, A. J., Ployhart, R. E., & Hale, D. (2023). Human capital resources: Reviewing the first decade and establishing a foundation for future research. Journal of Management, 49(1), 280-324. https://doi.org/10.1177/01492063221085912
notes: Recent review of the first decade of human capital resources research.

apa_citation: McClelland, D. C. (1973). Testing for competence rather than for intelligence. American Psychologist, 28(1), 1-14. https://doi.org/10.1037/h0034092
notes: Classic source for competence-based assessment beyond intelligence testing.

apa_citation: Spencer, L. M., & Spencer, S. M. (1993). Competence at work: Models for superior performance. Wiley.
notes: Classic HR competency source for knowledge/skills, motives, traits, self-concept, and layered competency logic.

apa_citation: Korthagen, F. A. J. (2004). In search of the essence of a good teacher: Towards a more holistic approach in teacher education. Teaching and Teacher Education, 20(1), 77-97. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.tate.2003.10.002
notes: Onion model of professional development; use only as supplementary layered-structure evidence, not as the main HR source.
